title: Multiple-choice cloze
description: Read the text below and decide which answer (A, B, C or D) best fits each gap.

content.text: In recent years, the concept of “digital minimalism” has gained traction among psychologists and sociologists. Early commentators (0) it as a passing trend, but growing evidence suggests it reflects a deeper shift in how people relate to technology.||Digital minimalism does not (1) abandoning devices altogether; rather, it encourages individuals to (2) their use of technology so that it serves clearly defined values. Supporters argue that this approach can (3) feelings of anxiety and distraction that often accompany constant connectivity.||One key principle is to be highly selective about online activities. Instead of (4) mindlessly through social media feeds, adherents choose a limited number of platforms that genuinely (5) their goals. This intentionality can help users (6) the temptation to check their phones whenever they experience a moment of boredom.||Critics, however, warn that digital minimalism may (7) those who lack the resources or flexibility to disconnect, such as gig workers who depend on apps for income. Nevertheless, its advocates maintain that even small adjustments can (8) a noticeable improvement in wellbeing, provided they are sustained over time.

example.explanation: La expresión “dismissed it as a passing trend” es una colocación fija. “Framed” y “enacted” no encajan con ‘trend’ en este sentido, y “appointed” se usa para cargos o personas, no para ideas.

questions.explanation: La expresión correcta es “does not entail abandoning devices”. “Entail” se usa para indicar que algo implica otra cosa. “Revolve” y “portray” no funcionan con ‘devices’, y “abandon” cambiaría el significado.
“Curate their use of technology” es una colocación moderna frecuente. “Scatter” y “seize” no encajan semánticamente, y “resume” implicaría volver a algo, no gestionarlo cuidadosamente.

La colocación es “alleviate feelings of anxiety”. “Heighten” y “trigger” implican aumentarlas, lo contrario del sentido, y “foster feelings of anxiety” también sería negativo, contrario al mensaje del texto.
La expresión fija es “scrolling mindlessly through social media feeds”. “Drifting” y “roaming” no se usan con ‘feeds’ en este contexto, y “pacing” se refiere a caminar, no a uso de pantallas.
“Support their goals” es la colocación natural. “Comply their goals” es gramaticalmente incorrecto, “collide” implica conflicto y “offset” se usa con costes o efectos, no con ‘goals’.
La expresión es “resist the temptation”. “Surrender the temptation” no es idiomático, “embrace” sería lo contrario (aceptar la tentación) y “pursue” tampoco encaja con ‘temptation’ en este contexto.

El texto sugiere que puede perjudicar a ciertos grupos: “may marginalise those who lack the resources…”. “Overlook” se referiría a ignorar, “empower” es positivo y “burden” no encaja tan bien con la idea de exclusión social.

“Bring about a noticeable improvement” es una colocación estándar. “Cope with an improvement” no tiene sentido, “account for” se usa para explicar algo y “set off” suele referirse a desencadenar reacciones, no a mejoras sostenidas.
